fields.name: HSION
fields.description: HSI clock enable Set and cleared by software. Set by hardware to force the HSI to ON when the product leaves Stop mode, if STOPWUCK = 1 or STOPKERWUCK = 1. Set by hardware to force the HSI to ON when the product leaves Standby mode or in case of a failure of the HSE which is used as the system clock source. This bit cannot be cleared if the HSI is used directly (via SW mux) as system clock, or if the HSI is selected as reference clock for PLL1 with PLL1 enabled (PLL1ON bit set to 1).

fields.name: HSIRDY
fields.description: HSI clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the HSI oscillator is stable.

fields.name: HSIKERON
fields.description: HSI clock enable in Stop mode Set and reset by software to force the HSI to ON, even in Stop mode, in order to be quickly available as kernel clock for peripherals. This bit has no effect on the value of HSION.

fields.name: HSIDIV
fields.description: HSI clock divider Set and reset by software. These bits allow selecting a division ratio in order to configure the wanted HSI clock frequency. The HSIDIV cannot be changed if the HSI is selected as reference clock for at least one enabled PLL (PLLxON bit set to 1). In that case, the new HSIDIV value is ignored.

fields.name: HSIDIVF
fields.description: HSI divider flag Set and reset by hardware. As a write operation to HSIDIV has not an immediate effect on the frequency, this flag indicates the current status of the HSI divider. HSIDIVF goes immediately to 0 when HSIDIV value is changed, and is set back to 1 when the output frequency matches the value programmed into HSIDIV.

enum_values.name: NotPropagated
enum_values.description: New HSIDIV ratio has not yet propagated to hsi_ck

enum_values.name: Propagated
enum_values.description: HSIDIV ratio has propagated to hsi_ck

fields.name: CSION
fields.description: CSI clock enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable CSI clock for system and/or peripheral. Set by hardware to force the CSI to ON when the system leaves Stop mode, if STOPWUCK = 1 or STOPKERWUCK = 1. This bit cannot be cleared if the CSI is used directly (via SW mux) as system clock, or if the CSI is selected as reference clock for PLL1 with PLL1 enabled (PLL1ON bit set to 1).

fields.name: CSIRDY
fields.description: CSI clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the CSI oscillator is stable. This bit is activated only if the RC is enabled by CSION (it is not activated if the CSI is enabled by CSIKERON or by a peripheral request).

fields.name: CSIKERON
fields.description: CSI clock enable in Stop mode Set and reset by software to force the CSI to ON, even in Stop mode, in order to be quickly available as kernel clock for some peripherals. This bit has no effect on the value of CSION.

fields.name: HSI48ON
fields.description: HSI48 clock enable Set by software and cleared by software or by the hardware when the system enters to Stop or Standby mode.

fields.name: HSI48RDY
fields.description: HSI48 clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the HSI48 oscillator is stable.

fields.name: HSEON
fields.description: HSE clock enable Set and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware to stop the HSE when entering Stop or Standby mode. This bit cannot be cleared if the HSE is used directly (via SW mux) as system clock, or if the HSE is selected as reference clock for PLL1 with PLL1 enabled (PLL1ON bit set to 1).

fields.name: HSERDY
fields.description: HSE clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the HSE oscillator is stable.

fields.name: HSEBYP
fields.description: HSE clock bypass Set and cleared by software to bypass the oscillator with an external clock. The external clock must be enabled with the HSEON bit to be used by the device. The HSEBYP bit can be written only if the HSE oscillator is disabled.

enum_values.name: Bypassed
enum_values.description: HSE crystal oscillator bypassed with external clock

fields.name: HSECSSON
fields.description: HSE clock security system enable Set by software to enable clock security system on HSE. This bit is 'set only' (disabled by a system reset or when the system enters in Standby mode). When HSECSSON is set, the clock detector is enabled by hardware when the HSE is ready and disabled by hardware if an oscillator failure is detected.

fields.name: HSEEXT
fields.description: external high speed clock type in Bypass mode Set and reset by software to select the external clock type (analog or digital). The external clock must be enabled with the HSEON bit to be used by the device. The HSEEXT bit can be written only if the HSE oscillator is disabled.

fields.name: PLL1ON
fields.description: PLL1 enable Set and cleared by software to enable PLL1. Cleared by hardware when entering Stop or Standby mode. Note that the hardware prevents writing this bit to 0, if the PLL1 output is used as the system clock.

fields.name: PLL1RDY
fields.description: PLL1 clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the PLL1 is locked.

fields.name: PLL2ON
fields.description: PLL2 enable Set and cleared by software to enable PLL2. Cleared by hardware when entering Stop or Standby mode.

fields.name: PLL2RDY
fields.description: PLL2 clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the PLL is locked.

fields.name: HSICAL
fields.description: HSI clock calibration Set by hardware by option byte loading during system reset nreset. Adjusted by software through trimming bits HSITRIM. This field represents the sum of engineering option byte calibration value and HSITRIM bits value.

fields.name: HSITRIM
fields.description: HSI clock trimming Set by software to adjust calibration. HSITRIM field is added to the engineering option bytes loaded during reset phase (FLASH_HSI_OPT) in order to form the calibration trimming value. HSICAL = HSITRIM + FLASH_HSI_OPT. After a change of HSITRIM it takes one system clock cycle before the new HSITRIM value is updated Note: The reset value of the field is 0x40.

fields.name: HSI48CAL
fields.description: Internal RC 48 MHz clock calibration Set by hardware by option-byte loading during system reset NRESET. Read-only.

fields.name: CSICAL
fields.description: CSI clock calibration Set by hardware by option byte loading during system reset NRESET. Adjusted by software through trimming bits CSITRIM. This field represents the sum of engineering option byte calibration value and CSITRIM bits value.

fields.name: CSITRIM
fields.description: CSI clock trimming Set by software to adjust calibration. CSITRIM field is added to the engineering option bytes loaded during reset phase (FLASH_CSI_OPT) in order to form the calibration trimming value. CSICAL = CSITRIM + FLASH_CSI_OPT. Note: The reset value of the field is 0x20.

fields.name: SW
fields.description: system clock and trace clock switch Set and reset by software to select system clock and trace clock sources (sys_ck). Set by hardware in order to: - force the selection of the HSI or CSI (depending on STOPWUCK selection) when leaving a system Stop mode - force the selection of the HSI in case of failure of the HSE when used directly or indirectly as system clock others: reserved

fields.name: SWS
fields.description: system clock switch status Set and reset by hardware to indicate which clock source is used as system clock. 000: HSI used as system clock (hsi_ck) (default after reset). others: reserved

enum_values.name: HSI
enum_values.description: HSI oscillator used as system clock

enum_values.name: CSI
enum_values.description: CSI oscillator used as system clock

enum_values.name: HSE
enum_values.description: HSE oscillator used as system clock

fields.name: STOPWUCK
fields.description: system clock selection after a wakeup from system Stop Set and reset by software to select the system wakeup clock from system Stop. The selected clock is also used as emergency clock for the clock security system (CSS) on HSE. 0: HSI selected as wakeup clock from system Stop (default after reset) STOPWUCK must not be modified when CSS is enabled (by HSECSSON bit) and the system clock is HSE (SWS = 10) or a switch on HSE is requested (SW =10).

enum_values.name: HSI
enum_values.description: HSI selected as wake up clock from system Stop

enum_values.name: CSI
enum_values.description: CSI selected as wake up clock from system Stop

fields.name: STOPKERWUCK
fields.description: kernel clock selection after a wakeup from system Stop Set and reset by software to select the kernel wakeup clock from system Stop.

fields.name: RTCPRE
fields.description: HSE division factor for RTC clock Set and cleared by software to divide the HSE to generate a clock for RTC. Caution: The software must set these bits correctly to ensure that the clock supplied to the RTC is lower than 1 MHz. These bits must be configured if needed before selecting the RTC clock source. ...

fields.name: TIMPRE
fields.description: timers clocks prescaler selection This bit is set and reset by software to control the clock frequency of all the timers connected to APB1 and APB2 domains.

enum_values.name: DefaultX2
enum_values.description: Timer kernel clock equal to 2x pclk by default

enum_values.name: DefaultX4
enum_values.description: Timer kernel clock equal to 4x pclk by default

fields.name: MCO1PRE
fields.description: MCO1 prescaler Set and cleared by software to configure the prescaler of the MCO1. Modification of this prescaler may generate glitches on MCO1. It is highly recommended to change this prescaler only after reset, before enabling the external oscillators and the PLLs. ...

fields.name: MCO1SEL
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output 1 Set and cleared by software. Clock source selection may generate glitches on MCO1. It is highly recommended to configure these bits only after reset, before enabling the external oscillators and the PLLs. others: reserved

fields.name: MCO2PRE
fields.description: MCO2 prescaler Set and cleared by software to configure the prescaler of the MCO2. Modification of this prescaler may generate glitches on MCO2. It is highly recommended to change this prescaler only after reset, before enabling the external oscillators and the PLLs. ...

fields.name: MCO2SEL
fields.description: microcontroller clock output 2 Set and cleared by software. Clock source selection may generate glitches on MCO2. It is highly recommended to configure these bits only after reset, before enabling the external oscillators and the PLLs. others: reserved

registers.name: CFGR2
registers.description: RCC CPU domain clock configuration register 2

fields.name: HPRE
fields.description: AHB prescaler Set and reset by software to control the division factor of rcc_hclk. Changing this division ratio has an impact on the frequency of all bus matrix clocks 0xxx: rcc_hclk = sys_ck (default after reset)

fields.name: PPRE1
fields.description: APB low-speed prescaler (APB1) Set and reset by software to control the division factor of rcc_pclk1. The clock is divided by the new prescaler factor from 1 to 16 cycles of rcc_hclk after PPRE write. 0xx: rcc_pclk1 = rcc_hclk1 (default after reset)

fields.name: PPRE2
fields.description: APB high-speed prescaler (APB2) Set and reset by software to control APB high-speed clocks division factor. The clocks are divided with the new prescaler factor from 1 to 16 APB cycles after PPRE2 write. 0xx: rcc_pclk2 = rcc_hclk1

fields.name: PPRE3
fields.description: APB low-speed prescaler (APB3) Set and reset by software to control APB low-speed clocks division factor. The clocks are divided with the new prescaler factor from 1 to 16 APB cycles after PPRE3 write. 0xx: rcc_pclk3 = rcc_hclk1

fields.name: AHB1DIS
fields.description: AHB1 clock disable This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the AHB1 peripherals from RCC_AHB1ENR are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_AHB1ENR. When this bit is set, all the AHB1 peripherals clocks from RCC_AHB1ENR are off. enable control bits

fields.name: AHB2DIS
fields.description: AHB2 clock disable This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the AHB2 peripherals from RCC_AHB2ENR are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_AHB2ENR. When this bit is set, all the AHB2 peripherals clocks from RCC_AHB2ENR are off. enable control bits

fields.name: AHB4DIS
fields.description: AHB4 clock disable This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the AHB4 peripherals from RCC_AHB4ENR are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_AHB4ENR. When this bit is set, all the AHB4 peripherals clocks from RCC_AHB4ENR are off. enable control bits

fields.name: APB1DIS
fields.description: APB1 clock disable value This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the APB1 peripherals (except IWDG) are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_APB1ENR. When this bit is set, all the APB1 peripherals clocks are off, except for IWDG. control bits

fields.name: APB2DIS
fields.description: APB2 clock disable value This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the APB2 peripherals are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_APB2ENR. When this bit is set, all the APB2 peripherals clocks are off. control bits

fields.name: APB3DIS
fields.description: APB3 clock disable value.Set and cleared by software This bit can be set in order to further reduce power consumption, when none of the APB3 peripherals are used and when their clocks are disabled in RCC_APB3ENR. When this bit is set, all the APB3 peripherals clocks are off. control bits

registers.name: PLL1CFGR
registers.description: RCC PLL clock source selection register

fields.name: PLL1SRC
fields.description: DIVMx and PLLs clock source selection Set and reset by software to select the PLL clock source. These bits can be written only when all PLLs are disabled. In order to save power, when no PLL is used, the value of PLL1SRC must be set to '00'. 00: no clock send to DIVMx divider and PLLs (default after reset).

enum_values.name: None
enum_values.description: No clock sent to DIVMx dividers and PLLs

fields.name: PLL1RGE
fields.description: PLL1 input frequency range Set and reset by software to select the proper reference frequency range used for PLL1. This bit must be written before enabling the PLL1.

fields.name: PLL1FRACEN
fields.description: PLL1 fractional latch enable Set and reset by software to latch the content of FRACN1 into the sigma-delta modulator. In order to latch the FRACN1 value into the sigma-delta modulator, PLL1FRACEN must be set to 0, then set to 1. The transition 0 to 1 transfers the content of FRACN1 into the modulator.

enum_values.name: Reset
enum_values.description: Reset latch to transfer FRACN to the Sigma-Delta modulator

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: Set latch to transfer FRACN to the Sigma-Delta modulator

fields.name: PLL1VCOSEL
fields.description: PLL1 VCO selection Set and reset by software to select the proper VCO frequency range used for PLL1. This bit must be written before enabling the PLL1.

fields.name: PLL1M
fields.description: prescaler for PLL1 Set and cleared by software to configure the prescaler of the PLL1. The hardware does not allow any modification of this prescaler when PLL1 is enabled (PLL1ON = 1 or PLL1RDY = 1). In order to save power when PLL1 is not used, the value of DIVM1 must be set to 0. ... ...

fields.name: PLL1PEN
fields.description: PLL1 DIVP divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll1_p_ck output of the PLL1. This bit can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0). In order to save power, when the pll1_p_ck output of the PLL1 is not used, the pll1_p_ck must be disabled.

fields.name: PLL1QEN
fields.description: PLL1 DIVQ divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll1_q_ck output of the PLL1. In order to save power, when the pll1_q_ck output of the PLL1 is not used, the pll1_q_ck must be disabled. This bit can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0).

fields.name: PLL1REN
fields.description: PLL1 DIVR divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll1_r_ck output of the PLL1. To save power, DIVR1EN and DIVR1 bits must be set to 0 when the pll1_r_ck is not used. This bit can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0).

registers.name: PLL2CFGR
registers.description: RCC PLL clock source selection register

fields.name: PLL2SRC
fields.description: DIVMx and PLLs clock source selection Set and reset by software to select the PLL clock source. These bits can be written only when all PLLs are disabled. In order to save power, when no PLL is used, the value of PLL2SRC must be set to '00'.

enum_values.name: None
enum_values.description: No clock sent to DIVMx dividers and PLLs

fields.name: PLL2RGE
fields.description: PLL2 input frequency range Set and reset by software to select the proper reference frequency range used for PLL2. These bits must be written before enabling the PLL2.

fields.name: PLL2FRACEN
fields.description: PLL2 fractional latch enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll2_p_ck output of the PLL2. To save power, when the pll2_p_ck output of the PLL2 is not used, the pll2_p_ck must be disabled.

enum_values.name: Reset
enum_values.description: Reset latch to transfer FRACN to the Sigma-Delta modulator

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: Set latch to transfer FRACN to the Sigma-Delta modulator

fields.name: PLL2VCOSEL
fields.description: PLL2 VCO selection Set and reset by software to select the proper VCO frequency range used for PLL2. This bit must be written before enabling the PLL2.

fields.name: PLL2M
fields.description: prescaler for PLL2 Set and cleared by software to configure the prescaler of the PLL2. The hardware does not allow any modification of this prescaler when PLL2 is enabled (PLL2ON = 1 or PLL2RDY = 1). In order to save power when PLL2 is not used, the value of DIVM2 must be set to 0. ... ...

fields.name: PLL2PEN
fields.description: PLL2 DIVP divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll2_p_ck output of the PLL2. To save power, when the pll2_p_ck output of the PLL2 is not used, the pll2_p_ck must be disabled.

fields.name: PLL2QEN
fields.description: PLL2 DIVQ divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll2_q_ck output of the PLL2. To save power, when the pll2_q_ck output of the PLL2 is not used, the pll2_q_ck must be disabled.

fields.name: PLL2REN
fields.description: PLL2 DIVR divider output enable Set and reset by software to enable the pll2_r_ck output of the PLL2. To save power, DIVR2EN and DIVR2 bits must be set to 0 when the pll2_r_ck is not used.

fields.name: PLL1N
fields.description: Multiplication factor for PLL1VCO Set and reset by software to control the multiplication factor of the VCO. These bits can be written only when the PLL is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0). ... ... Others: reserved

fields.name: PLL1P
fields.description: PLL1 DIVP division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll1_p_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0). Note that odd division factors are not allowed. ...

fields.name: PLL1Q
fields.description: PLL1 DIVQ division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll1_q_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0). ...

fields.name: PLL1R
fields.description: PLL1 DIVR division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll1_r_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL1ON = 0 and PLL1RDY = 0). ...

fields.name: PLL1FRACN
fields.description: fractional part of the multiplication factor for PLL1 VCO Set and reset by software to control the fractional part of the multiplication factor of the VCO. These bits can be written at any time, allowing dynamic fine-tuning of the PLL1 VCO. The software must set correctly these bits to insure that the VCO output frequency is between its valid frequency range, that is: * 128 to 560 MHz if PLL1VCOSEL = 0 * 150 to 420 MHz if PLL1VCOSEL = 1 VCO output frequency = Fref1_ck x (PLL1N + (PLL1FRACN / 213)), with * PLL1N between 8 and 420 * PLL1FRACN can be between 0 and 213- 1 * The input frequency Fref1_ck must be between 1 and 16 MHz. To change the PLL1FRACN value on-the-fly even if the PLL is enabled, the application must proceed as follows: * Set the bit PLL1FRACEN to 0 * Write the new fractional value into PLL1FRACN * Set the bit PLL1FRACEN to 1

fields.name: PLL2N
fields.description: Multiplication factor for PLL2VCO Set and reset by software to control the multiplication factor of the VCO. These bits can be written only when the PLL is disabled (PLL2ON = 0 and PLL2RDY = 0). ... ... Others: reserved

fields.name: PLL2P
fields.description: PLL2 DIVP division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll2_p_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL2 is disabled (PLL2ON = 0 and PLL2RDY = 0). ...

fields.name: PLL2Q
fields.description: PLL2 DIVQ division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll2_q_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL2 is disabled (PLL2ON = 0 and PLL2RDY = 0). ...

fields.name: PLL2R
fields.description: PLL2 DIVR division factor Set and reset by software to control the frequency of the pll2_r_ck clock. These bits can be written only when the PLL1 is disabled (PLL2ON = 0 and PLL2RDY = 0). ...

fields.name: PLL2FRACN
fields.description: fractional part of the multiplication factor for PLL2 VCO Set and reset by software to control the fractional part of the multiplication factor of the VCO. These bits can be written at any time, allowing dynamic fine-tuning of the PLL2 VCO. The software must set correctly these bits to insure that the VCO output frequency is between its valid frequency range, that is: * 128 to 560 MHz if PLL2VCOSEL = 0 * 150 to 420 MHz if PLL2VCOSEL = 1 VCO output frequency = Fref2_ck x (PLL2N + (PLL2FRACN / 213)), with * PLL2N between 8 and 420 * PLL2FRACN can be between 0 and 213- 1 * The input frequency Fref2_ck must be between 1 and 16 MHz. To change the PLL2FRACN value on-the-fly even if the PLL is enabled, the application must proceed as follows: * Set the bit PLL2FRACEN to 0 * Write the new fractional value into PLL2FRACN * Set the bit PLL2FRACEN to 1

registers.name: CIER
registers.description: RCC clock source interrupt enable register

fields.name: LSIRDYIE
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the LSI oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: LSERDYIE
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the LSE oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: CSIRDYIE
fields.description: CSI ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the CSI oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: HSIRDYIE
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the HSI oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: HSERDYIE
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the HSE oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: HSI48RDYIE
fields.description: HSI48 ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the HSI48 oscillator stabilization.

fields.name: PLL1RDYIE
fields.description: PLL1 ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by PLL1 lock.

fields.name: PLL2RDYIE
fields.description: PLL2 ready interrupt enable Set and reset by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by PLL2 lock.

registers.name: CIFR
registers.description: RCC clock source interrupt flag register

fields.name: LSIRDYF
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing LSIRDYC bit. Set by hardware when the LSI clock becomes stable and LSIRDYIE is set.

fields.name: LSERDYF
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing LSERDYC bit. Set by hardware when the LSE clock becomes stable and LSERDYIE is set.

fields.name: CSIRDYF
fields.description: CSI ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing CSIRDYC bit. Set by hardware when the CSI clock becomes stable and CSIRDYIE is set.

fields.name: HSIRDYF
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing HSIRDYC bit. Set by hardware when the HSI clock becomes stable and HSIRDYIE is set.

fields.name: HSERDYF
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing HSERDYC bit. Set by hardware when the HSE clock becomes stable and HSERDYIE is set.

fields.name: HSI48RDYF
fields.description: HSI48 ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing HSI48RDYC bit. Set by hardware when the HSI48 clock becomes stable and HSI48RDYIE is set.

fields.name: PLL1RDYF
fields.description: PLL1 ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing PLL1RDYC bit. Set by hardware when the PLL1 locks and PLL1RDYIE is set.

fields.name: PLL2RDYF
fields.description: PLL2 ready interrupt flag Reset by software by writing PLL2RDYC bit. Set by hardware when the PLL2 locks and PLL2RDYIE is set.

fields.name: HSECSSF
fields.description: HSE clock security system interrupt flag Reset by software by writing HSECSSC bit. Set by hardware in case of HSE clock failure.

enum_values.name: NoInterrupt
enum_values.description: No clock security interrupt caused by HSE clock failure

enum_values.name: Interrupt
enum_values.description: Clock security interrupt caused by HSE clock failure

registers.name: CICR
registers.description: RCC clock source interrupt clear register

fields.name: LSIRDYC
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear LSIRDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: LSERDYC
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear LSERDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: CSIRDYC
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear CSIRDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: HSIRDYC
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear HSIRDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: HSERDYC
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear HSERDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: HSI48RDYC
fields.description: HSI48 ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear HSI48RDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: PLL1RDYC
fields.description: PLL1 ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear PLL1RDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: PLL2RDYC
fields.description: PLL2 ready interrupt clear Set by software to clear PLL2RDYF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: HSECSSC
fields.description: HSE clock security system interrupt clear Set by software to clear HSECSSF. Reset by hardware when clear done.

fields.name: GPDMA1RST
fields.description: GPDMA1 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: GPDMA2RST
fields.description: GPDMA2 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.description: CRC block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: RAMCFGRST

fields.name: GPIOBRST

fields.name: GPIOCRST

fields.name: GPIODRST

fields.name: GPIOHRST
fields.description: GPIOH block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: ADCRST
fields.description: ADC block reset

fields.name: DAC12RST
fields.description: DAC block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: HASHRST
fields.description: HASH block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: RNGRST
fields.description: RNG block reset Set and reset by software.

registers.name: APB1LRSTR
registers.description: RCC APB1 peripheral low reset register

fields.name: TIM2RST
fields.description: TIM2 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: TIM3RST
fields.description: TIM3 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: TIM6RST
fields.description: TIM6 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: TIM7RST
fields.description: TIM7 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: OPAMPRST
fields.description: OPAMP block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: SPI2RST
fields.description: SPI2 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: SPI3RST
fields.description: SPI3 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: COMPRST
fields.description: COMP block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: USART2RST
fields.description: USART2 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: USART3RST
fields.description: USART3 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: I2C1RST
fields.description: I2C1 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: I2C2RST
fields.description: I2C2 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: I3C1RST
fields.description: I3C1 block reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: LSEON
fields.description: LSE oscillator enabled Set and reset by software.

fields.name: LSERDY
fields.description: LSE oscillator ready Set and reset by hardware to indicate when the LSE is stable. This bit needs 6 cycles of lse_ck clock to fall down after LSEON has been set to 0.

fields.name: LSEBYP
fields.description: LSE oscillator bypass Set and reset by software to bypass oscillator in debug mode. This bit must not be written when the LSE is enabled (by LSEON) or ready (LSERDY = 1)

enum_values.name: Bypassed
enum_values.description: LSE crystal oscillator bypassed with external clock

fields.name: LSEDRV
fields.description: LSE oscillator driving capability Set by software to select the driving capability of the LSE oscillator. These bit can be written only if LSE oscillator is disabled (LSEON = 0 and LSERDY = 0).

enum_values.name: MediumLow
enum_values.description: Medium low LSE oscillator driving capability

enum_values.name: MediumHigh
enum_values.description: Medium high LSE oscillator driving capability

fields.name: LSECSSON
fields.description: LSE clock security system enable Set by software to enable the clock security system on 32 kHz oscillator. LSECSSON must be enabled after LSE is enabled (LSEON enabled) and ready (LSERDY set by hardware) and after RTCSEL is selected. Once enabled, this bit cannot be disabled, except after a LSE failure detection (LSECSSD = 1). In that case the software must disable LSECSSON.

enum_values.name: SecurityOff
enum_values.description: Clock security system on 32 kHz oscillator off

enum_values.name: SecurityOn
enum_values.description: Clock security system on 32 kHz oscillator on

fields.name: LSECSSD
fields.description: LSE clock security system failure detection Set by hardware to indicate when a failure has been detected by the clock security system on the external 32 kHz oscillator.

enum_values.name: NoFailure
enum_values.description: No failure detected on 32 kHz oscillator

fields.name: LSEEXT
fields.description: low-speed external clock type in bypass mode Set and reset by software to select the external clock type (analog or digital). The external clock must be enabled with the LSEON bit, to be used by the device. The LSEEXT bit can be written only if the LSE oscillator is disabled.

fields.name: RTCSEL
fields.description: RTC clock source selection Set by software to select the clock source for the RTC. These bits can be written only one time (except in case of failure detection on LSE). These bits must be written before LSECSSON is enabled. The VSWRST bit can be used to reset them, then it can be written one time again. If HSE is selected as RTC clock, this clock is lost when the system is in Stop mode or in case of a pin reset (NRST).

enum_values.name: LSE
enum_values.description: LSE oscillator clock used as RTC clock

enum_values.name: LSI
enum_values.description: LSI oscillator clock used as RTC clock

enum_values.name: HSE
enum_values.description: HSE oscillator clock divided by a prescaler used as RTC clock

fields.name: RTCEN
fields.description: RTC clock enable Set and reset by software.

fields.name: VSWRST
fields.description: VSwitch domain software reset Set and reset by software.

fields.name: LSCOEN
fields.description: Low-speed clock output (LSCO) enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSCOSEL
fields.description: Low-speed clock output selection Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSION
fields.description: LSI oscillator enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSIRDY
fields.description: LSI oscillator ready Set and cleared by hardware to indicate when the LSI oscillator is stable. After the LSION bit is cleared, LSIRDY goes low after three internal low-speed oscillator clock cycles. This bit is set when the LSI is used by IWDG or RTC, even if LSION = 0.

fields.name: RMVF
fields.description: remove reset flag Set and reset by software to reset the value of the reset flags.

fields.name: PINRSTF
fields.description: pin reset flag (NRST) Reset by software by writing the RMVF bit. Set by hardware when a reset from pin occurs.

fields.name: BORRSTF
fields.description: BOR reset flag Reset by software by writing the RMVF bit. Set by hardware when a BOR reset occurs (pwr_bor_rst).

fields.name: SFTRSTF
fields.description: system reset from CPU reset flag Reset by software by writing the RMVF bit. Set by hardware when the system reset is due to CPU.The CPU can generate a system reset by writing SYSRESETREQ bit of AIRCR register of the core M33.

fields.name: IWDGRSTF
fields.description: independent watchdog reset flag Reset by software by writing the RMVF bit. Set by hardware when an independent watchdog reset occurs.

fields.name: WWDGRSTF
fields.description: window watchdog reset flag Reset by software by writing the RMVF bit. Set by hardware when a window watchdog reset occurs.

fields.name: LPWRRSTF
fields.description: Low-power reset flag Set by hardware when a reset occurs due to Stop or Standby mode entry, whereas the corresponding nRST_STOP, nRST_STBY option bit is cleared. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: PRIV
fields.description: RCC functions privilege configuration

enum_values.name: Any
enum_values.description: RCC functions can be modified by privileged or unprivileged access

enum_values.name: PrivilegedOnly
enum_values.description: RCC functions can only be modified by privileged access
